category_title: Lower-achieving students with reactive study habits
student_description: This student struggles with last-minute cramming, neglecting sleep, and taking excessive, unstructured breaks (relying on them 92% of the time). They react emotionally to poor grades rather than adjusting their habits. They uniquely require anonymous peer data to evaluate their habits without feeling forced to confess, alongside highly specific parameters for break management and study timelines.

criteria.rationale: Sustained use of objective data helps transition the student from reactionary, emotional responses after poor exams to proactive, analytical planning.
criteria.check: The tutor's long-term plan includes recurring metacognitive reflection exercises that use accessible data to analyze the causes of academic difficulty.

criteria.rationale: To break the cycle of night-before cramming, the student needs explicit, ongoing guidance on exactly when to deploy study strategies.
criteria.check: The tutor establishes a specific timeline for exam preparation (e.g., starting a few days before, but not more than a week) rather than just suggesting what to study.

criteria.rationale: Reliable, quick communication and resource sharing outside of sessions builds the student's confidence and reduces anxiety leading up to exams.
criteria.check: The tutor incorporates a system for quick out-of-session communication (e.g., fast email replies) and provides concrete resources like study guides.
